methods.description: Process for examining and photographing fossil skeletons and otoliths using a stereomicroscope and SEM imaging for detailed analysis.

steps.description: The fossil skeletons and otoliths were examined and photographed under a Leica M165 FC stereomicroscope equipped with a digital camera (Leica DC 200).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine and photograph fossils using a Leica M165 FC stereomicroscope with a digital camera.

steps.description: Otoliths preserved in situ in the paratypes were carefully extracted and stored separately; the right sagitta of each paratype and the lapillus were used for SEM imaging.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract otoliths and prepare for SEM imaging.

methods.description: Measurement and analysis of morphometric parameters and meristic characters of skeletons and otoliths using ImageJ.

steps.description: The morphometric parameters of the skeletons and otoliths correspond to those used in previous publications on extant goby species.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure morphometric parameters of skeletons and otoliths.

steps.description: All measurements were recorded to the nearest 0.1 mm using ImageJ and were normalized based on the standard length (SL) of the measured fish.
steps.searchable_summary: Record and normalize measurements using ImageJ.

steps.description: Meristic characters comprised counts of abdominal and caudal vertebrae, counts of fin rays, and determination of the pterygiophore formula of the first dorsal fin.
steps.searchable_summary: Count meristic characters and determine pterygiophore formula.
